rawFields.Back of Card (sound): Definition: a vowel or vowel team followed by the letter r, resulting in a distintive sound. R-controlled vowels are also known as "bossy r" words.

Definition:
An open syllable ends with a long vowel sound spelled with a single vowel; there is no consonant following it. A few words are open syllables. .

Definition:
When the vowel of a word or syllable is short, the syllable will be closed off by one or more consonants.

Definition:
Consonant-le combinitations are only found at the ends of words. Start at the end and count back - 1,2,3 divide.

The schwa is the most common vowel sound in English.
All vowels can make the schwa sound.
It sounds like short u or short i.
It's in words with more than one syllable in the quiet syllable.
It's too lazy to sit up!
Definition: VCe words and syllables contain long vowels spelled with a single letter, followed by a single consonant, and a silent e.

Definition:
A vowel team may be two, three, or four letters;it can represent a long, short, or diphthong vowel sound.
